content:
- Reviewed docs/src across tutorial/how-to/reference/explanation, task .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md, and Diataxis source files in .agents/skills/diataxis/references.
- Conclusion: passes=true for task 01 looks premature. Coverage exists in all four quadrants, but not yet at the task bar of "operator no longer needs to ask what is still missing here?".
- Main gaps: tutorial section is still too thin and one tutorial is more conceptual than guided; how-to section still has at least one mixed-mode page and meta documentation-process notes; explanation section has only two pages and leaves major operator-facing concepts uncovered; reference is the strongest section but still does not by itself make the whole docs set feel complete.
- Useful examples: observing-failover tutorial lacks concrete commands/expected outputs for several steps; check-cluster-health includes source-capture caveats instead of finished operator guidance; switchover/how-to is decent but still narrow overall section coverage.
- Next if continuing: report pass=false recommendation with file-specific reasoning and suggest the missing pages / improvements by Diataxis quadrant.

- Used ask-k2 skill on the full docs/src corpus only, with explicit instruction not to use code or external context.
- K2 answer: docs set is not complete enough; tutorials weakest, how-to underdeveloped, explanation/reference stronger but still incomplete. It suggested missing operational docs like add/remove node, TLS rotation, monitoring, restore/rejoin, partition handling, metrics/glossary/troubleshooting references.
- For task hardening, the best non-prescriptive additional requirement is a blind docs-only corpus review gate: before passes=true, ask K2 over full docs/src only and require that it does NOT identify any quadrant as thin/underdeveloped or any obvious missing operator-significant doc areas.
- This is better than fixed required page topics because it enforces outcome quality without over-prescribing exact docs inventory.
